string.text: Combat thins the Zone faster than the engine refills it. AlifeBalance tracks deaths per (level, faction) and advances the respawn cooldown of an eligible smart on that level by a fraction of its cooldown each wave. After the configured number of waves the cooldown expires and the engine spawns one squad from the smart's own configured pool on its next alife tick. It does not spawn anything itself. Works alongside vanilla, ZCP, Redone, and any other spawner mod.
Master toggle. When off, no death tracking and no cooldown advances.

How many waves are needed to fully advance a smart's respawn cooldown. A wave fires when one squad's worth of deaths has accumulated for a (level, faction) pair, and subtracts 1/waves of the picked smart's respawn cooldown. At 1, one wave clears the full cooldown (instant refill). At 4 (default), four waves of accumulated combat refill the smart. At 8, the refill is paced across eight waves. Higher values mean refills feel more earned by sustained combat.

Floor on the per-wave cooldown advance, in game hours. Protects against a high wave count or short respawn_idle producing an advance so small that the full wave count accomplishes nothing. With min=1 hour and a 12-hour cooldown, the effective wave cap is 12. With min=6 hours and the same cooldown, the effective wave cap is 2. Default 1 hour. Raise to slow the pacing further.

Place a green PDA map marker on every smart terrain that receives a wave. The marker lingers for 5 real-time minutes, then clears. Right-click any marker to teleport to that smart or show its stats. Independent of log level.
Debug and development tools. DEBUG level enables structured tracing of every wave to alifebalance.log. Has performance cost.
Controls log verbosity. ERROR: crashes only. WARN: anomalies. INFO: startup and config only. DEBUG: every death, wave, eligible-set compute, gate outcome, and tick summary.
Display total deaths, counted deaths (faction + level resolved), protected and trash-tier filtered, scan ticks, waves fired, and engine spawns observed.

Clear all death counters, eligibility and threshold caches, per-smart wave stats, and diagnostic counters. Existing cooldown advances already written to smart-terrain timestamps persist; the engine resolves them on its next try_respawn.
